This is the Architectural Shadow.

We dress up our biases as "best practices." We force Kubernetes onto static sites because it makes us feel safe. We mistake our comfort for pragmatism.

When a system becomes too brittle to move, you have to be able to "Phoenix" it from scratch.

Kenny Ang has good examples with AWS EKS, including troubleshooting webhook errors and handling ALB health checks under STRICT mode. You should also take a look at the Ambient Mesh approach for this without sidecars. (5/5)

The article walks through installing Istio via Helm (istio-base and istiod charts), three methods for sidecar injection (namespace-level with labels, pod-level annotations, or istioctl kube-inject). (4/5)

man-in-the-middle attacks, and identity spoofing. Mutual TLS addresses this by requiring both parties to authenticate while encrypting traffic in transit.

One approach to deal with this uses the #Istio service mesh with Envoy sidecar proxies. This approach removes the (2/5)

https://lckhd.eu/gLtsLy

Many people are surprised to hear that Pod-to-pod communication in #Kubernetes isn't encrypted by default. Without #mTLS, clusters remain vulnerable to traffic sniffing, 🧵

And of course, if you're looking for a vendor, Datadog has you covered.

You can join the preview for the OpenTelemetry eBPF profiler here:

docs.datadoghq.com/profiler/ena...

If this sounds interesting to you, and you'd like to get an early taste of the troubleshooting powers of the Profiles Signal, please check out our post on the OpenTelemetry blog for more details: 

opentelemetry.io/blog/2026/pr...

Profiles (unlike logs, metrics and traces) are directly linked to code.

Florian Lehner and I demonstrated this at KubeCon by using profiling to root cause an incident caused by a coding agent.

But the reverse works too: agents can use profiles to troubleshoot or optimize code.

The OpenTelemetry Profiles Signal is now in Alpha 🚀

This is a big step towards making Continuous Profiling in production available to the whole industry.

And given the rise of AI coding, this couldn't come at a better moment in time ... 🧵

The energy from the community continues to grow every KubeCon.
